title: After Death Sentence Overturned by Supreme Court, Karim Khojasteh Sentenced to Prison
content:
According to HRANA, Karim Khojasteh has been sentenced to imprisonment.

After the Supreme Court overturned his death sentence and referred the case to a parallel branch for retrial, Khojasteh was sentenced to 12 years and six months in prison. The ruling was issued recently by Branch 1 of the Rasht Revolutionary Court.

In December 2025, the same court had sentenced him to death on the charge of baghi (armed rebellion against the regime) through alleged membership in an opposition group.

Karim Khojasteh was arrested by security forces at his workplace on March 13, 2025, and transferred to Lakan Prison in Rasht.

He had previously been arrested on July 20, 2024, due to his critical activities and was released on bail of 500 million tomans on November 29, 2024.

Karim Khojasteh is a resident of Khomam County, located in the northern province of Gilan.
snippet: According to HRANA, Karim Khojasteh has been sentenced to imprisonment.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Execution of Death Sentences of Two Prisoners, Including a Juvenile Offender, in Iranshahr Prison
content:
According to a report by HRANA, citing Haal Vsh, two prisoners, including a juvenile offender, were executed by hanging in Iranshahr Prison.

Based on the report, at dawn on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, the death sentence of Abdolvahed Sangari was carried out at the prison. He had been arrested in 2019 in Zahedan following a physical altercation that resulted in a murder charge and was subsequently sentenced to death by the judiciary. He had been transferred to solitary confinement on Tuesday, July 21, in preparation for his execution.

In a separate case, Meysam Irandegani was executed at dawn on Tuesday, July 21, at the same prison. According to Haal Vsh, he had been arrested approximately four years and two months earlier on a murder charge when he was only 14 years old. He was initially transferred to the Juvenile Correction and Rehabilitation Center in Zahedan before eventually being moved to Iranshahr Prison. His death sentence was later issued by the judicial authorities.

At the time of writing, the executions of these two prisoners had not been officially announced by prison authorities or the relevant state institutions.
snippet: According to a report by HRANA, citing Haal Vsh, two prisoners, including a juvenile offender, were executed by hanging in Iranshahr Prison.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests: Supreme Court Upholds Death Sentence of Ahad Shokouhian
content:
According to HRANA, the death sentence of Ahad Shokouhian has been upheld.

The ruling was confirmed by Branch 9 of the Supreme Court. Earlier, Branch 26 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Iman Afshari, sentenced Shokouhian to three death sentences on charges including acting against national security, assembly and collusion to act against national security, destruction of public property, and participation in the killing of two security force personnel. According to information received by HRANA, the allegations against him include participating in the January 8 protests in Pakdasht and setting fire to a mosque.

A source familiar with the case and close to Shokouhian’s family told HRANA that he was not present at the mosque when the fire occurred and was arrested solely because he bore a resemblance to another individual identified as the alleged perpetrator. Nevertheless, his sentence was issued and upheld without a thorough review of the available surveillance camera footage, and both the investigating judge and the trial judge refused requests to re-examine the recordings.
snippet: According to HRANA, the death sentence of Ahad Shokouhian has been upheld.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Escalation of Judicial Crackdown on Protesters: A Review of the Most Significant Cases from the Past Two Weeks
content:
According to HRANA, over the past two weeks, judicial proceedings in a number of cases related to the January 2026 protests and other security-related cases have entered a new phase, marked by the issuance and implementation of death sentences, the commencement of trials, and growing concerns over the condition of detainees.

The cases reviewed by HRANA span Isfahan, Shahrud, Mashhad, Tehran, Shiraz, Mahallat, Karaj, and Tabriz. They are accompanied by persistent allegations of fair trial violations, including denial of effective access to legal counsel, forced confessions, court hearings held inside prisons or conducted remotely, and a lack of transparency regarding the evidence relied upon by the courts. HRANA’s findings indicate that at least seven prisoners in protest- and security-related cases were executed during this period.
snippet: According to HRANA, over the past two weeks, judicial proceedings in a number of cases related to the January 2026 protests and other security-related cases have entered a new phase, marked by the
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Ghezel Hesar Prisoners’ Hunger Strike Enters Tenth Day
content:
According to HRANA, the hunger strike by prisoners in Ward 2 of Ghezel Hesar Prison, which began on Monday, July 13, 2026, after six prisoners sentenced to death were transferred to solitary confinement, continued today.

Based on information received by HRANA, the prisoners gathered in the ward hall on Wednesday and once again refused to receive prison meals, marking the tenth day of their hunger strike. The protesting prisoners stated their opposition to the implementation of death sentences, particularly in cases involving drug-related offenses.

In recent days, videos recorded inside Ghezel Hesar Prison have also emerged, showing scenes from the prisoners’ hunger strike and sit-in.

The protests began on Monday, July 13, following the transfer of six prisoners sentenced to death in drug-related cases to solitary confinement. The move prompted a collective response from prisoners in Ward 2, who have continued their hunger strike and sit-in ever since.

It is worth noting that HRANA also reported last year on similar hunger strikes by a number of prisoners at Ghezel Hesar Prison protesting the implementation of death sentences.
snippet: According to HRANA, the hunger strike by prisoners in Ward 2 of Ghezel Hesar Prison, which began on Monday, July 13, 2026, after six prisoners sentenced to death were transferred to solitary
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Political Prisoner Amirhassan Akbari Monfared Sentenced to Death
content:
According to HRANA, 27-year-old Amirhassan Akbari Monfared has been sentenced to death.

According to a verdict issued by Branch 15 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati, and served on Mr. Akbari Monfared two days ago, he was sentenced to death on the charge of baghi (armed rebellion) through alleged membership in the People’s Mojahedin Organization of Iran (MEK). He is currently imprisoned in Evin Prison and had previously stood trial before the same court.

Amirhossein Akbari Monfared was arrested alongside his father, Mohammad Akbari Monfared, and his brother and sister, Amir hossein and Zeinab Akbari Monfared, by Ministry of Intelligence agents in Karaj during the winter of 2025. The three other family members were later released on bail.

So far, no information has been made available regarding the details of the charges brought against the other members of the family. However, a source previously informed HRANA that they are facing security-related charges.

Mohammad Akbari Monfared, aged approximately 58, Amirhassan Akbari Monfared, 33, Zeinab Akbari Monfared, 30, and Amirhossein Akbari Monfared, 27, are residents of Karaj.
snippet: According to HRANA, 27-year-old Amirhassan Akbari Monfared has been sentenced to death.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Baha’i Citizen Arman Misaghian Sentenced to Two Years in Prison
content:
According to HRANA, Arman Misaghian, a Baha’i citizen, has been sentenced to prison.

Based on information received by HRANA, Branch 29 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court sentenced Arman Misaghian to one year of discretionary imprisonment on the charge of “propaganda against the regime” and an additional one year on the charge of “insulting the Supreme Leader.” As supplementary punishment, the court also banned him for two years from driving, signing commercial documents, and membership in groups.

The case was presided over by Judge Seyed Ali Mazloum.

So far, no information has been made available regarding the specific conduct underlying the charges brought against this citizen.

Over the past decade, Iran’s Baha’i community has been subjected to more security-related and judicial persecution than any other religious minority in the country. According to HRA’s annual reports, an average of 62.41% of all reported violations of the rights of religious minorities during the past year involved violations of the rights of Baha’i citizens.
snippet: According to HRANA, Arman Misaghian, a Baha’i citizen, has been sentenced to prison.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests: Mahnam Navab Safavi Sentenced to Death
content:
According to HRANA, Mahnam Navab Safavi, one of the detainees from the nationwide protests of January 2026, has been sentenced to death.

Based on information obtained by HRANA, Branch 5 of the Revolutionary Court of Isfahan, presided over by Judge Hemati-Nejad, sentenced Mr. Navab Safavi to death on charges including moharebeh through alleged involvement in “the destruction of public property,” “propaganda against the regime,” “assembly and collusion against national security,” and “encouraging people to commit murder.”

A source informed and close to the family of this citizen confirmed the news in a conversation with HRANA, stating that one of the alleged acts cited against Mr. Navab Safavi was writing slogans.

Mahnam Navab Safavi, 22, is a Persian-language rapper and a resident of Isfahan. He was arrested on January 10, 2026, during the nationwide protests in the Sheikh Sadough area of Isfahan and was later transferred to Dastgerd Prison in this city.
snippet: According to HRANA, Mahnam Navab Safavi, one of the detainees from the nationwide protests of January 2026, has been sentenced to death.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Younes Nabizadeh Sentenced to 10 Years in Prison
content:
According to a report by Kolber News, Younes Nabizadeh has been sentenced to imprisonment.

Based on this report, Branch 1 of the Revolutionary Court of Urmia, presided over by Judge Najafzadeh, sentenced this citizen to 10 years and one day in prison on the charge of alleged membership in one of the opposition groups.

Previously, Mr. Nabizadeh’s court hearing was held via video conference in the same judicial branch.

In June 2024, Nabizadeh was arrested by security forces in Urmia and was later transferred to one of the Ministry of Intelligence’s detention facilities in this city.

The report did not provide further details regarding this citizen’s current place of detention.

Younes Nabizadeh, 37, is married and the father of two children. He is originally from Delo village in the Dizaj Dol region of Urmia County.
snippet: According to a report by Kolber News, Younes Nabizadeh has been sentenced to imprisonment.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests: Mohammad Abed-Raftar Sentenced to 21 Years in Prison
content:
According to HRANA, Mohammad Abed-Raftar has been sentenced to 21 years in prison.

According to a ruling issued and served on July 2, 2026, by Branch 47 of the Isfahan Province Court of Appeals, Abed-Raftar was sentenced to one year in prison for “propaganda against the regime”; five years for “incitement and encouragement to wage war and commit killings”; five years in prison for “assembly and collusion to commit crimes against national security” and Ten years for “attempting to manufacture unconventional devices (Molotov cocktails) with the intent of cooperating with hostile states.”

As an additional punishment, he was also sentenced to a two-year ban on residing in Isfahan Province.

According to information received by HRANA, his case has now been referred to the sentence enforcement office.

On April 26, 2026, Abed-Raftar was tried before Branch 102 of the Shahin Shahr and Meymeh Criminal Court Two. On the same day, the court sentenced him to 21 years in prison and a permanent ban on residing in Isfahan Province. The ruling was issued by Javad Kamali, the judge responsible for handling cases within the jurisdiction of the Revolutionary Court in Shahin Shahr.
snippet: According to HRANA, Mohammad Abed-Raftar has been sentenced to 21 years in prison.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests: Mehdi Khanki Executed
content:
Mizan, Iran’s Judiciary’s Media Center, announced today that Mehdi Khanki, one of those arrested in connection with the January 2026 protests, has been executed. According to Mizan, a branch of the Karaj Revolutionary Court had sentenced him to death and the confiscation of all his property under the Law on Intensifying Punishments for Espionage and Cooperation with the Zionist Regime and Hostile States Against National Security and Interests.
The charges against him included “carrying out operational activities on behalf of Israel, the United States, and hostile groups” and “manufacturing, obtaining, and possessing firearms and military ammunition, and using them.” The Judiciary stated that his sentence was carried out after being upheld by the Supreme Court.
snippet: Mizan, Iran’s Judiciary’s Media Center, announced today that Mehdi Khanki, one of those arrested in connection with the January 2026 protests, has been executed. According to Mizan, a branch of the
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Two Women Sentenced to Prison, Flogging, and Internal Exile in Qorveh
content:
According to HRANA, citing Kolbar News, two citizens in Qorveh have been sentenced to prison, flogging, and internal exile.

According to the report, Branch 102 of the Qorveh Criminal Court convicted Neda Dadehjani and Setayesh (Behnoosh) Sarabi on the charge of “disrupting public order and peace.” Each was sentenced to one year in prison, 31 lashes, and two years of internal exile to Nik Shahr County.

In a separate part of their case, Branch One of the Qorveh Revolutionary Court convicted both women of “assembly and collusion to commit crimes against national security,” sentencing each to an additional two years in prison.

Based on the final ruling, each woman received a sentence of three years’ imprisonment, 31 lashes, and two years of internal exile. Under the final judgment, two years of each prison sentence are enforceable.

The two women were arrested by security forces in connection with the nationwide protests held in January 2026.

Neda Dadehjani, who is active in theater, had previously won an award at a regional theater festival for persons with disabilities. Setayesh (Behnoosh) Sarabi is a resident of Qorveh.
snippet: According to HRANA, citing Kolbar News, two citizens in Qorveh have been sentenced to prison, flogging, and internal exile.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: January 2026 Protests: Supreme Court Overturns Death Sentence of Ali Fattah
content:
According to HRANA, the Supreme Court has overturned the death sentence imposed on Ali Fattah (Kamali), one of the detainees arrested during the January 2026 protests.

Based on information received by HRANA, the Supreme Court overturned the political prisoner’s death sentence some time ago.

A source familiar with the case told HRANA: “Following the onset of serious mental health issues, Ali Fattah (Kamali) is currently hospitalized at Razi Psychiatric Teaching and Treatment Hospital (Aminabad). He had previously been transferred to the same facility once before to receive medical treatment.”

Ali Fattah (Kamali) was arrested in Tehran on January 12, 2026, during the nationwide protests. Holding a Malaysian student residence permit, he had returned to Iran only a few days before his arrest.

Following his arrest, he was held at Greater Tehran Penitentiary before being transferred to Ghezel Hesar Prison in Karaj in late June.

In May of this year, Branch 26 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Iman Afshari, sentenced him to death on the charge of “enmity against God” (moharebeh).
snippet: According to HRANA, the Supreme Court has overturned the death sentence imposed on Ali Fattah (Kamali), one of the detainees arrested during the January 2026 protests.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency

title: Baha’i Citizen Shideh Tavakkoli Sentenced to Prison and Additional Punishments
content:
According to HRANA, Shideh Tavakkoli has been sentenced to imprisonment and additional punishments.

According to the ruling issued by Branch 15 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court, presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati, Ms. Tavakkoli was sentenced to 10 years in prison, permanent deprivation of employment in government and public service, and a fine of 3.3 billion rials (330 million tomans) on charges of membership in anti-state groups and propaganda activities contrary to Islamic law. She was also sentenced to one additional year in prison on the charge of propaganda against the state.

The court further ordered the confiscation of two properties belonging to her in Tehran, valued at 18.7 billion tomans, as well as part of her personal belongings, including electronic devices, books, and religious items.

The verdict was issued by the court on May 20, 2026, and served on Ms. Tavakkoli on May 26, 2026.

Shideh Tavakkoli was arrested by security forces at her home in Tehran on May 27, 2025. After the completion of interrogations, she was temporarily released from prison on bail.
snippet: According to HRANA, Shideh Tavakkoli has been sentenced to imprisonment and additional punishments.
sourceDescription: Human Rights Activists News Agency Hrana – News Agency | Human Rights Activists News Agency
